era: 1984-2000 full run. Grunge. E=7. Ellensburg WA origin. SST Records 1987-1991. Epic Records 1992-2000. Nearly Lost You. Sweet Oblivion. Dust.
associated_musicians: Mad Season 2011 reunion (Lanegan sang for Staley)

theory_nodes.concept: Geographic outlier: Ellensburg, 100 miles east of Seattle over the Cascades. High desert, CWU, Kittitas Valley. A band that chose to reach across the mountains. Grunge as PNW-wide phenomenon, not just Seattle.
theory_nodes.description: Ellensburg as the engine's most geographically displaced major band.

theory_nodes.concept: Mark Lanegan: baritone deep as grief. Blues lineage (Cash, Orbison, Young) not metal/punk. Born Ellensburg 1964, died Killarney Ireland 2022, age 57. The most important voice from outside Seattle in the grunge era.
theory_nodes.description: Lanegan's voice as a different American tradition in the Seattle grunge moment.

theory_nodes.concept: 'Nearly Lost You' (1992): Singles soundtrack, #1 Mainstream Rock, Billboard Hot 100 #34. Sweet Oblivion as breakthrough. Lee Conner's climbing riff + Lanegan's vocal at full cry.
theory_nodes.description: The song that made the Screaming Trees known outside the underground.

theory_nodes.concept: Barrett Martin bridge: drums in Screaming Trees (307) AND Mad Season (305, 224). The human infrastructure connecting two of the grunge cluster's most important entries. Two degrees apart in the engine.
theory_nodes.description: Barrett Martin as the engine's most direct band-to-band human bridge in the current zone.

cross_references.relationship: Barrett Martin drums in both. Two degrees apart. The human bridge between Mad Season and Screaming Trees in the engine.
First Mad Season appearance where Barrett Martin was first introduced in engine context.
Aberdeen enters at 308. Ellensburg (307) + Aberdeen (308): both bands from outside Seattle that were central to the scene. Geographic parallel.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=8. The salmon run overwhelms. Multi-species aggregate sound of thousands of fish: the ecology outweighs the music. E=15 combined.
